nodes.standard_name: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
nodes.original_text_name: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
nodes.context: Far-right survivalist anti-government militia advocating Christian Identity, active in the US in the 1970s-1980s.
Baptist congregation that evolved into the CSA.
Far-right survivalist community founded by Robert G. Millar.
White supremacist organization advocating Christian Identity, based in Hayden Lake, Idaho.

nodes.standard_name: Mountain Church of Jesus Christ the Savior
nodes.original_text_name: Mountain Church of Jesus Christ the Savior
nodes.context: Extreme right-wing church founded by Robert E. Miles in Cohoctah, Michigan.
White supremacist hate group with which CSA had close ties.
White supremacist revolutionary group formed in Idaho, advocating overthrow of US government.
White supremacist prison gang with links to CSA.
Outlaw motorcycle club that provided training to CSA in exchange for weapons.
Far-right anti-government militia movement active in the US, associated with tax protestors.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: CSA developed from the Zarephath-Horeb Community Church.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: CSA founder James Ellison moved to Elohim City after prison and married the founder's granddaughter.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: Ellison was mentored by Aryan Nations founder Richard Butler and had close ties to the group.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.target: Mountain Church of Jesus Christ the Savior
edges.context: Ellison was mentored by Robert E. Miles, founder of the Mountain Church.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: Ellison had close ties to the Ku Klux Klan.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: CSA had links to The Order; members traveled between groups and a stolen vehicle from The Order was found at CSA compound.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: CSA had links to the Aryan Brotherhood, a white supremacist prison gang.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: Pagans provided training in booby traps and survival techniques in return for weapons and ammunition.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: CSA member Leonard Ginter hosted a meeting that led to the killing of Posse Comitatus member Gordon Kahl, and Ellison revered Kahl as a hero.
